body:
I noticed a regression:

1. Create a sticky note and write line long enough to wrap one the next line. Confirm.
2. Edit again and delete the text, then write a single-line text. Confirm.
3. The text is misaligned.

	![excal-regr-sticky](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/148832258-f76f7a9f-656f-42a7-aa8e-ff3029848b85.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
> I noticed a regression:
> 
> 1. Create a sticky note and write line long enough to wrap one the next line. Confirm.
> 2. Edit again and delete the text, then write a single-line text. Confirm.
> 3. The text is misaligned.
>    ![excal-regr-sticky](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/148832258-f76f7a9f-656f-42a7-aa8e-ff3029848b85.gif)

Good catch!. Updated the implementation to cache `originalText` and comparing with editor value instead. Added spec for this case. Should be fine now.

body: I've also added  `window` `focus` event. This will catch cases of having both tabs visible or partially visible.
author: dwelle

body:
The failing tests were caused because blurring the wysiwyg was for some reason firing `change` event on `window`, which in turn overwritten the elements with empty `[]` because in tests we don't persist state to LS.

But this makes me wonder — the `change` event we're now listening for will be triggered in many cases like picking a file from the filesystem, thus potentially overwriting the state with stale data in case the LS isn't updated in time. This could happen, since dialogs are generally thread blocking. Also could happen once we move to IDB which is async.

Also note that even `visibilitychange` alone could be affected by this.
author: dwelle

body: Another, related thing: we need to disable the syncing when collaborating, because we're not syncing to LS during collab.
author: dwelle

body:
> I've also added `window` `focus` event. This will catch cases of having both tabs visible or partially visible.

Why do we need `focus` event ? coz this will trigger LS sync when ever window gains focus even when visible eg switching focus from devtools to Excalidraw window, also the `visibilityChange` will be triggered when ever the page becomes fully visible from partial visible/minimised state as well.
author: ad1992

body:
> Why do we need `focus` event ? coz this will trigger LS sync when ever window gains focus even when visible eg switching focus from devtools to Excalidraw window, also the `visibilityChange` will be triggered when ever the page becomes fully visible from partial visible/minimised state as well.

`visibilityChange` will give us only a subset of `focus` (except for one special case). We want to handle cases like when you have both tabs visible (on one or two monitors) and switch between them.

Either way, we want to make the syncing robust enough so we don't have to think whether `change` event can cause race conditions. It must work properly no matter what.
author: dwelle

author: vercel

body:
I checked, and realized this is kinda a hot path, not just for excalidraw.com, but for host apps as well. For example, we call `updateScene` in collaboration on every update, and pass in the whole scene elements. Restoring them unnecessarily can quickly become a bottleneck (excalidraw collaboration syncing is already super heavy and congests easily as it is).

1. We can make this optional, but I'd rather avoid this — it wouldn't really solve the issue. 
2. Second option is to optimize the restoring calls.

	This would likely consist of versioning of some kind so we can bail if the version is up to date.
3. Third option is to call restore manually before passing data to `updateScene`, in places where the data comes from external boundaries where we're not reasonably certain they are already restored.

@zsviczian @ad1992
author: dwelle

body: I think best would be to come up with (2) to optimise restore calls, (3) is upto the host if they want to restore they always can and we can recommend it in readme as well to make sure migrations work properly
author: ad1992

body:
> I think best would be to come up with (2) to optimise restore calls, (3) is upto the host if they want to restore they always can and we can recommend it in readme as well to make sure migrations work properly

Well, if we wanna put the responsibility onto hosts, then there's not (that much) need for (2).

body: Strangely, the hitbox doesn't work when the fill is `solid` --- unclear what's happening
author: dwelle

body:
Found out why this is happening. It's because when the fill style is solid most of the curve path ops of the filled shape have op value as "lineTo" which is not yet handled in the `hitTestRoughShape` function. 

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/24bf4cb5fb6f7028f11796b1cb39506976d274e0/src/element/collision.ts#L899-L900

I came up with something that somewhat works which I'll push shortly. Trying to understand how this all works to come up with a proper solution.
![firefox_ERnEsYuAUr](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22396000/150680036-f9853d53-d3fb-48a2-9971-5babc09a505d.gif)

@ad1992 @lipis @dwelle
While I was creating this issue template form, an idea came up for how to improve the bug submission quality.

In other frontend projects, it's easy to suggest codesandbox.io to create an issue. However, for excalidraw I'm not sure what the best way to collect a reproducible bug is. I was thinking:
- suggest a user to share a file to their exported `.excalidraw` file or recommend the user to upload the contents of their excalidraw file to a github gist file with a `.json` ending (I think this might be the most reasonable method + it's free)?

body:
This is very nice.
I would recommend adding the link property to all elements, not just text elements.
What will be the trigger for the hyperlink dialog to appear? or will it always pop up above the object when the object is selected?
How will hyperlinks operate in View mode?
author: zsviczian

body:
> This is very nice. I would recommend adding the link property to all elements, not just text elements. What will be the trigger for the hyperlink dialog to appear? or will it always pop up above the object when the object is selected? How will hyperlinks operate in View mode?

The state it's in now doesn't represent the latest design, which we'll push here soon.

In short, what we're going for right now is this:

1. by default, the popup will not show for any element until you create the link ↓
2. you create the link by clicking a link icon from the sidebar menu, which will open the popup over the element
3. once the initial popup is confirmed and the link created, the popup will then show when you select the element (on `pointerup`, for reasons below)
4. when the popup is shown and you start moving/resizing/etc. the element, the popup is hidden to not distract/hinder the editing action (this is why we initially show the popup on `pointerup` instead of `pointerdown` so that there's no popup flicker when you start moving the shape without releasing your pointer button)

All elements will support hyperlinks.
author: dwelle

body:
Great!
One more thought. Currently, all links are prefixed with `https://`. This should only be done when a link is entered without specifying the protocol. Besides internal obsidian links, I can imagine links to local resources `file://` or remote resources `ftp://` or in some cases non-secure `http://`.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/149993273-78475622-22ed-4231-89b9-eb6170b01a9d.mp4

author: ad1992

body:
🔥

Here's a UX note: when moving/resizing, after you stop, the popup blocks the view which is distracting or prevents you from visually aligning to other elements, and or prevents you from selecting those occluded elements.

I suggest we don't show the popup if you've previously moved/resized the element (maybe other cases too), we shouldn't show the popup until you click the element again. (though there should be some threshold like 5px because you often make micro-movements when clicking elements).

I think that when you interact with the hyperlinked element in this way, 99% of the time you will not want to edit the hyperlink — and if you do, you can click on the element again to show the popup.

—

Alternatively, if we think the popup is too distracting in general, we could theoretically make it only show on clicking the external-link icon (therefore make the icon not redirect to the site in edit mode).

Hard to say, but we should take Excalidraw's editing experience philosophy into account here and think on this.

![excal-hyperlinks-move-annoying](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/150388138-e8ff2ac7-1050-4b01-b94f-749c43257ed7.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
A few notes on the link-icon: 

1. I think we'll want to put a timeout on the tooltip hover as well as there's the same UX issue as with the hyperlink popup: it becomes distracting when moving mouse over the canvas elements that are hyperlinked. Especially since the case for needing to constantly review where the link goes to is even smaller than for showing the popup.
2. We should only open the url if the `pointerup` event is in the same area (size of the icon) as `pointerdown`. This will fix (among other) a case where you happen to start dragging while in the hitbox area of the link icon.
3. The hitbox seems too large. Right now it's around the size of the red rectangle below, which ends up in false positives when you want to select the element (or neighboring elements):

	![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/152020516-f34eac9b-78d3-44cc-9598-4fc70e3c5bed.png)

4. I don't think the link-icon should be on top of elements with higher z-index:

	(applies to interactivity as well)

	![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/152021340-0863e24c-9d00-4b36-a72b-ed5f24ee8c8c.png)
author: dwelle

body:
There are 2 bugs around editor popup (likely related):

1. when you select a non-hyperlinked element and then hover over a hyperlinked element, it shows a link editor popup on top of the selected element:

	![excal-hyperlinks-popup-editor-bug1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/152022383-4b6aa539-7490-428f-8896-bf46fa57538a.gif)

2. when you select a hyperlinked element, then switch to a drawing tool and draw a new element elsewhere on the canvas, it again shows the editor popup

	![excal-hyperlinks-popup-editor-bug2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/152022504-9feda45b-da50-4191-bc1d-a5c54fe6cf27.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
> 1. I think we'll want to put a timeout on the tooltip hover as well as there's the same UX issue as with the hyperlink popup: it becomes distracting when moving mouse over the canvas elements that are hyperlinked. Especially since the case for needing to constantly review where the link goes to is even smaller than for showing the popup.

User will hover over the link icon only when they want to visit the link or review it so why do we need a timeout for showing the tooltip in that case ? Also we have timeout for hiding the popover, IOW we show it immediately as soon as user hovers over the element

>2.  We should only open the url if the pointerup event is in the same area (size of the icon) as pointerdown. This will fix (among other) a case where you happen to start dragging while in the hitbox area of the link icon.

I am not sure what you mean as user will not be able to drag when hovered in hitbox area of link icon so how will this check help ?

author: vercel

body:
@ad1992, this simplifies and resolves the errors (and heuristic "magic") with text positioning in textwysiwyg. This change greatly improves the user experience for Excalidraw when it is rendered embedded in an app with offsetLeft & offsetTop !=0.

Please take a look and let's push this important improvement to production.
author: zsviczian

body: This is a great improvement @zsviczian ❤️.  Could you also write specs for this ? Will do one round of testing and then should be good to merge.
author: ad1992

body:
> This is a great improvement @zsviczian ❤️. Could you also write specs for this ? Will do one round of testing and then should be good to merge.

Great! A second (and 3rd, 4th...) round of testing is always essential. What do you mean by specs?
author: zsviczian

body:
> > This is a great improvement @zsviczian ❤️. Could you also write specs for this ? Will do one round of testing and then should be good to merge.
> 
> Great! A second (and 3rd, 4th...) round of testing is always essential. What do you mean by specs?

I meant tests for the change just to make sure we don't break in future as this part has been broken often in the past.
author: ad1992

body:
> > > This is a great improvement @zsviczian ❤️. Could you also write specs for this ? Will do one round of testing and then should be good to merge.
> > 
> > 
> > Great! A second (and 3rd, 4th...) round of testing is always essential. What do you mean by specs?
> 
> I meant tests for the change just to make sure we don't break in future as this part has been broken often in the past.

I think this broke frequently in the future because there was a formula error. sceneCoordsToViewportCoords was wrong, and we were trying to resolve its impact by applying complex calculations in textwysiwyg.  That should be resolved for good.

Regarding testing... I don't really know how to test for zoom and offset situations... I haven't yet looked at excalidraw tests, I'll take a look to see if this sort of testing can be automated.  My recommendation would be to deploy a second app with every change, the second should have offsets and righthand side margin.
author: zsviczian

body:
> > > > This is a great improvement @zsviczian ❤️. Could you also write specs for this ? Will do one round of testing and then should be good to merge.
> > > 
> > > 
> > > Great! A second (and 3rd, 4th...) round of testing is always essential. What do you mean by specs?
> > 
> > 
> > I meant tests for the change just to make sure we don't break in future as this part has been broken often in the past.
> 
> I think this broke frequently in the future because there was a formula error. sceneCoordsToViewportCoords was wrong, and we were trying to resolve its impact by applying complex calculations in textwysiwyg. That should be resolved for good.
> 
> Regarding testing... I don't really know how to test for zoom and offset situations... I haven't yet looked at excalidraw tests, I'll take a look to see if this sort of testing can be automated.

Sure 👍🏻 .  The tests would probably be tricky for this but lets give it a try and decide accordingly :).

>My recommendation would be to deploy a second app with every change, the second should have offsets and righthand side margin.

What do you mean by deploying an app with offsets and right handed margin ? We have the same code sandbox example running with package example in port 3001 which can be used for testing
author: ad1992

body:
> What do you mean by deploying an app with offsets and right handed margin ? We have the same code sandbox example running with package example in port 3001 which can be used for testing

To make the question concrete. If I would want to test a PR with screen offsets, how can I do that. For sake of example, the current preview release for the hyperlinks PR is https://excalidraw-f13roaq8h-excalidraw.vercel.app/ I tried https://excalidraw-f13roaq8h-excalidraw.vercel.app:3001/ but that did not bring up anything...
author: zsviczian

body:
> > What do you mean by deploying an app with offsets and right handed margin ? We have the same code sandbox example running with package example in port 3001 which can be used for testing
> 
> To make the question concrete. If I would want to test a PR with screen offsets, how can I do that. For sake of example, the current preview release for the hyperlinks PR is https://excalidraw-f13roaq8h-excalidraw.vercel.app/ I tried https://excalidraw-f13roaq8h-excalidraw.vercel.app:3001/ but that did not bring up anything...

Ohh ok I meant it will work in [`localhost:3001`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/README_NEXT.md#development), we can also deploy it as well so its available in preview too
author: ad1992

body:
> Ohh ok I meant it will work in [`localhost:3001`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/README_NEXT.md#development), we can also deploy it as well so its available in preview too

That would be great! I was looking around the repository but didn't yet have the patience to figure it out. If you give me some pointers I am also happy to propose a PR with port 3001 being available in preview too.


BTW Have you had time to test this fix? I've deployed this to the Obsidian Excalidraw build a week ago and have not experienced any issues since. I am pretty confident that this is working as expected. Would be good to get this deployed before further functions build on the current erroneous sceneCoordsToViewportCoords() function.
author: zsviczian

body: For sure, let's merge! Thanks!

body: @dwelle, please take a look at this when you have time. I've been using this change on the iPad and it is a game-changer for freedraw.  This is the most straightforward solution I can find to the problem of palm rejection given the cross-platform issues of not being able to use the SPen or Pencil SDKs.
author: zsviczian

body:
Hey @zsviczian —  you've beat us to it!

But, I was thinking we should:

1. extend this all tools, not just freedraw 
2. auto-enable this on `pen` event detection (while still allowing to manually disable — although the exact behavior of what should happen in the future afterwards isn't specced out)
3. reject only `touch` events (allow `mouse` events) — though this is an edge case

Nevertheless, we can leave the above for later as users will benefit from even a partial solution.

But, I think we should put the button elsewhere. While it may make sense to put lock buttons together, it'd be too confusing. Also the jumping caused by the menu being centered is not good (though this is a separate issue and should be fixed regardless). On some tables, including iPad Air in portrait mode, the button also barely fits in.

I'd want to make the button more prominent, both by making it slightly bigger and placing it elsewhere so it's noticeable. Though I'm not sure where myself — perhaps top-right (below the host-supplied top-right UI, which on excalidraw.com is the E+ banner). 
Or maybe bottom left above the help icon. Ideas welcome.
author: dwelle

body:
> * extend this all tools, not just freedraw

I implemented palm rejection only for freedraw based on practical experience. I never had any issues with unwanted strokes with other tools, only freedraw. Also in case of freedraw I disabled pinch zoom, but kept gesture panning. This is also based on experience. Often when I start to draw, my hand touches the screen first causing excalidraw to zoom in or out. This is a problem because the relative size of my drawing, and thus the relative stroke width of the drawing is impacted. Panning however is seldom an issue, and very practical during freedraw.

> * auto-enable this on `pen` event detection (while still allowing to manually disable — although the exact behavior of what should happen in the future afterwards isn't specced out)

Good suggestion. This looks easy to implement.

On this note, I was looking at the touchTimout solution for the context menu, hoping something similar could be developed for pinch zooming… but I couldn’t come up with a solution that worked effectively in practice. This is because the accidental pinch zoom typically happens right at the beginning, when I start to draw, not during I draw… thus enabling pinch zoom based on a touchTimout timer is not a good idea, because I would have pinch zoom enabled just when I start to draw again.

> * reject only `touch` events (allow `mouse` events) — though this is an edge case

I can’t really think of a practical case when this might be an issue. Maybe a laptop with a stylus… though my experience with laptop styluses is overwhelmingly negative. 


placing the lock to the bottom right next to the help sounds good. I also toyed with the idea of having a separate pinch zoom lock next to the zoom, but thought better of it, and discarded the idea.
author: zsviczian

body:
Would you hide the pen lock button in zen mode?
I vote for yes.
author: zsviczian

body:
> I implemented palm rejection only for freedraw based on practical experience. I never had any issues with unwanted strokes with other tools, only freedraw.

I often have issues with at least line tool as well. I'm not sure whether it makes sense to limit this to only a subset of tools. I can imagine people have varying workflows (e.g. lock the tool and then draw many circles/squares while resting the palm).

> Also in case of freedraw I disabled pinch zoom, but kept gesture panning. This is also based on experience. Often when I start to draw, my hand touches the screen first causing excalidraw to zoom in or out. This is a problem because the relative size of my drawing, and thus the relative stroke width of the drawing is impacted. Panning however is seldom an issue, and very practical during freedraw.

I missed this because I didn't think to use 2 fingers to pan (in pen mode). I don't think many people will discover this on their own, but I suppose allowing panning with just one finger would cause issues again.

> On this note, I was looking at the touchTimout solution for the context menu, hoping something similar could be developed for pinch zooming… but I couldn’t come up with a solution that worked effectively in practice. This is because the accidental pinch zoom typically happens right at the beginning, when I start to draw, not during I draw… thus enabling pinch zoom based on a touchTimout timer is not a good idea, because I would have pinch zoom enabled just when I start to draw again.

Unclear — when does accidental pinch zooming happen for you? Unless you are talking when outside the pen mode?

> Would you hide the pen lock button in zen mode?

This is _after_ we auto-enable pen mode? If the auto-enable gets disabled (depends on how design it), then you'd not have any way to enable/disable in zen mode.
author: dwelle

body:
I left the button where it is, but I fixed the jumping tool island. I tried all corners, for me, this is the logical solution still.

I added penDetected to the appState. This is set to true the very first time the user starts to draw with the pen. That is when the pen lock appears.

I renamed penLock to penMode.

penDetected and penMode are reset every time the scene loads. 

penMode now applies equally to all the tools.

I change allowOnPointerDown to your suggestion
```typescript
const allowOnPointerDown =
      !this.state.penMode || event.pointerType !== "touch";
```

The pinch zoom issue happens outside pen mode. Thus I blocked pinch zoom in pen mode.
author: zsviczian

body:
> I left the button where it is, but I fixed the jumping tool island. I tried all corners, for me, this is the logical solution still.

It's logical, but there I still have 2 issues with it.

1. space. On my iPad it's already very crowded, even if we disregard that the top-right is cut off (I'd like to also move the dark mode toggle there later).
	
	Though the penMode button isn't the only issue and a larger redesign is needed.

	![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/151715719-ee4ed6a9-17ec-430f-8bad-5549e5aa4dab.png)

2. Even now, people don't know what the tool lock button is doing, and now we're adding another one next to it. Won't help.

But, I'm not gonna block it on this — we can tweak this as we go along.

> I added penDetected to the appState. This is set to true the very first time the user starts to draw with the pen. That is when the pen lock appears.
>
> penDetected and penMode are reset every time the scene loads.

Since it's a session state, can we move it out from appState to the `App` instance (you can pass the `penMode` prop to `LayerUI`)? We can always move it to the appState later when needed, which won't require a migration. In general, the default should be to put into `appState` only that which makes sense to persist (or the host app needs to track, but even then it may make more sense to just expose instead of put into AppState).

---

Progress looking great 👍
author: dwelle

body: We should prob exclude `selection` tool from the penMode. While it might make sense to include even that, right now it doesn't fully work anyway (e.g. you can't select, but you can drag).
author: dwelle

body:
> We should prob exclude `selection` tool from the penMode. While it might make sense to include even that, right now it doesn't fully work anyway (e.g. you can't select, but you can drag).

What do you mean? I don't think the `selection` tool is not affected by penMode. Or you want to hide the pen lock when the selection tool is active?
author: zsviczian

body:
> 2\. Even now, people don't know what the tool lock button is doing, and now we're adding another one next to it. Won't help.

The pen lock only appears when people start to draw with a pen.
- It will be easier to understand its purpose given the link with the user action: "hm... I started  to draw with a pen and this lock button appeared 🤔"
- It won't clutter the screen unless someone uses a pen.

> Since it's a session state, can we move it out from appState to the `App` instance (you can pass the `penMode` prop to `LayerUI`)? We can always move it to the appState later when needed, which won't require a migration. In general, the default should be to put into `appState` only that which makes sense to persist (or the host app needs to track, but even then it may make more sense to just expose instead of put into AppState).

penMode and penDetected now follow the same logic as elementLocked.



I think this should be ready to be merged.
author: zsviczian

body:
on the subject of lock buttons...
we could actually drop the element-lock button and implement triple-state element-type buttons instead. When the user presses the element type button twice, the icon changes to have a little lock in the top right corner.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/151922209-5d816596-cc29-4eb4-a207-f473470572fd.png)
author: zsviczian

body:
> on the subject of lock buttons...
> we could actually drop the element-lock button and implement triple-state element-type buttons instead. When the user presses the element type button twice, the icon changes to have a little lock in the top right corner.

Tldraw does something similar: double-click locks the tool. There are 2 problems: 1) it's completely undiscoverable 2) it's per-item. I think the setting should remain global so you don't have to constantly disable/enable on specific items.
author: dwelle

body:
> What do you mean? I don't think the `selection` tool is not affected by penMode. Or you want to hide the pen lock when the selection tool is active?

penMode currently does apply to `selection` tool, which is the problem because it's buggy. We can either 1) fix the bugs, or 2) disable the penMode for `selection`. I think (2) makes more sense for now, especially because it's unlikely that palm detection will be a problem for this tool (this is a similar argument to yours in terms of why should we enable to penMode for non-freedraw tools, but I'd argue that there's a difference in terms of use cases).
author: dwelle

body:
> > Since it's a session state, can we move it out from appState to the `App` instance (you can pass the `penMode` prop to `LayerUI`)? We can always move it to the appState later when needed, which won't require a migration. In general, the default should be to put into `appState` only that which makes sense to persist (or the host app needs to track, but even then it may make more sense to just expose instead of put into AppState).
> 
> penMode and penDetected now follow the same logic as elementLocked.

What I meant is: don't put the state into `AppState` at all. That said, upon further review it probably doesn't matter much, so let's leave it.
author: dwelle

body:
> What I meant is: don't put the state into `AppState` at all. That said, upon further review it probably doesn't matter much, so let's leave it.

That is what I thought as well... but my React skills weren't enough to figure out how to do that. Moving penMode and penDetected to props did not work, because props is read only. Also, based on my limited understanding you need to store variables in appState to achieve the dynamic rendering of components.
author: zsviczian

body:
> That is what I thought as well... but my React skills weren't enough to figure out how to do that. Moving penMode and penDetected to props did not work, because props is read only. Also, based on my limited understanding you need to store variables in appState to achieve the dynamic rendering of components.

We'd need to pass the instance prop to the `<LayerUI/>` compo as a prop for that. Anyway, it's fine to leave as is.

I think the only block to shipping is excluding `selection` tool from the penMode, if we agree.
author: dwelle

body:
> > That is what I thought as well... but my React skills weren't enough to figure out how to do that. Moving penMode and penDetected to props did not work, because props is read only. Also, based on my limited understanding you need to store variables in appState to achieve the dynamic rendering of components.
> 
> We'd need to pass the instance prop to the `<LayerUI/>` compo as a prop for that. Anyway, it's fine to leave as is.
> 
> I think the only block to shipping is excluding `selection` tool from the penMode, if we agree.

I resolved the selection tool issue. I think we are ready to merge! 🎉
author: zsviczian

body:
> I resolved the selection tool issue. I think we are ready to merge! 🎉

Thanks.

One last thing I just noticed. While in penMode, touching the canvas (`touch`) when a drawing tool is selected reverts to `selection` tool. Not sure if a recent regression or not.
author: dwelle

body:
> > I resolved the selection tool issue. I think we are ready to merge! 🎉
> 
> Thanks.
> 
> One last thing I just noticed. While in penMode, touching the canvas (`touch`) when a drawing tool is selected reverts to `selection` tool. Not sure if a recent regression or not.

I think simply returning if this check is `false` will do the trick: 

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4657/files#diff-86963609bec6fda3d34233676d5ae33f6aa4b9bbe2758a10cd4863b704b37093R2643-R2646
author: dwelle

body:
> in penMode, touching the canvas (`touch`) when a drawing tool is selected reverts to `selection` tool. Not sure if a recent regr

This feature is there on excalidraw.com as well. I thought it was intentional. All I did was to exclude the freedraw tool from this - again based on my personal experience that during freedraw this switch to selection tool happened frequently as I rest my hand on the screen while drawing.
author: zsviczian

body:
> This feature is there on excalidraw.com as well. I thought it was intentional.

No, what's happening here is we're not fully preventing `touch` events when penMode is enabled. IOW, palm prevention isn't working for tools other than `freedraw`. The reason why it works for `freedraw` right now is because we implicitly tool-lock when drawing with `freedraw`, whereas we don't for other tools.

So what we want to do is simply prevent `touch` events when penMode is on (and we're not using the `selection` tool).

The only question is whether the solution I proposed (return early) is the correct way to do it, but I think it is. I'll push the change so I can test it out on my tablet (I'm too lazy to set up local debugging session).
author: dwelle

body:
What about elementType==="image"?
or in pen mode, we expect the user to interact with the image with a pen?
author: zsviczian

body: also adding text... I think it would be natural to double tap the screen to add text even in pen mode
author: zsviczian

body:
> What about elementType==="image"?
> or in pen mode, we expect the user to interact with the image with a pen?
>
> also adding text... I think it would be natural to double tap the screen to add text even in pen mode

I'm not sure how any of these things relate to the change I pushed?

First off, `selection` still works even with `touch`. Second, `pen` isn't prevented.
author: dwelle

body:
> What about elementType==="image"?

Note that on touch screens the image insertion is not handled via touch, it's pasted directly.
author: dwelle

body: @zsviczian the latest works well for me. I think we can ship and improve on later. Are you facing any issues?
author: dwelle

body:
> orks well for me. I think we can ship and improve on later. Are you facing any issues?

overall works ok for me as well. My only question/concern is handling of text elements. I typically type with my finger, not the pen, thus double touch for creating text comes natural.  I just pushed a change to see how it works...

```typescript
    const allowOnPointerDown =
      !this.state.penMode ||
      event.pointerType !== "touch" ||
      this.state.elementType === "selection" ||
      this.state.elementType === "text";
```
author: zsviczian

body: With this last change to allow elementType === "text" the solution works well for me!
author: zsviczian

body:
Allowing text tool is fine. I've allowed `image` as well, just to be safe (though ideally it shouldn't matter).

🚀 ❤️

body:
This is a good improvement. I tested it on iPad with pencil and touch and on Samsung Note with touch and S-Pen. Works as expected.

Not related to this change... but do you have any idea why the context menu won't on a long press with the apple pencil, but does show with a long press with the S-Pen. This difference is the same for both the current excalidraw.com version and this PR.
author: zsviczian

body:
> Not related to this change... but do you have any idea why the context menu won't on a long press with the apple pencil, but does show with a long press with the S-Pen. This difference is the same for both the current excalidraw.com version and this PR.

Good catch. AFAIK iOS doesn't support touch-hold to open contextMenu, so we're detecting it manually. We should include pens in that. ~~It also means this PR doesn't handle iOS touch — gonna fix.~~ never mind, the check is downstream so it applies.
author: dwelle

body:
> What is the purpose of this?
> 
> ```ts
> event.nativeEvent.pointerType === "pen" &&
>           event.button !== POINTER_BUTTON.SECONDARY
> ```

Allow opening contextmenu via the pen button that usually is used for opening contextmenus (it usually emits the same `event.button` type as right-click on a mouse).

I'll add a doc.
author: dwelle

body:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4675/commits/50a67ccf1cf7d084a5da8fd471f8641b7a5e6487

this fix will require more work because of how it's implemented. I'll remove the commit for now as it actually needs timeout clearing which I'm not doing for pen. I'll open a new PR for this fix later.

body:
Also, I think we should re-measure the text after unbound so that it ends up with the bounding box you get once you edit the text in the future.

One last thing: what about also supporting it the other way round — when select a text element and a container, support `bind text to container`? Maybe for another PR.
author: dwelle

body:
> should we have an action in sidebar ?

I would not, for now. Let's conserve UI buttons. We can decide on that later based on feedback.

done

Yep lets keep for anothe PR

body:
I've rewritten the `onLinkOpen` flow to use the standard behavior of doing `event.preventDefault()` if you wish to handle the redirecting yourself. This has two benefits: 1) clarity 2) you don't have to reimplement excalidraw's behavior for external redirects.

We've also stopped passing `link` since it can be retrieved from `element.link` /cc @ad1992

author: vercel

body:
Cool. One thing: we should disable click-through if you drag between `pointerdown` and `pointerup`, if the drag distance is above some threshold (e.g. the `DRAGGING_THRESHOLD` we're using). Otherwise it will open links if you start dragging and release over a link. Can happen often when the links are on elements, not text.

![excal-view-mode-link-drag-bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/152766511-faf3c9be-7b45-4f0f-b32b-5afcb41c5ff8.gif)

(This affects edit mode as well, but to a much smaller degree since the clickable hitboxes are only the small icons.)
author: dwelle

body:
> DRAGGING_THRESHOLD

I am instead checking the pointer down and pointer up coords are exactly same as for click event it should be same then only redirect
author: ad1992

body:
> I am instead checking the pointer down and pointer up coords are exactly same as for click event it should be same then only redirect

exact match is too strict. I tend to make micro movements a lot when clicking using a mouse.
author: dwelle

body:
> > I am instead checking the pointer down and pointer up coords are exactly same as for click event it should be same then only redirect
> 
> exact match is too strict. I tend to make micro movements a lot when clicking using a mouse.

But when its a click the pointer events will be same else it means that there was some movement?
author: ad1992

body:
> But when its a click the pointer events will be same else it means that there was some movement?

There is a tiny movement, yes. Hence the `DRAGGING_THRESHOLD` (10px), which we consider as unintentional.
author: dwelle

body:
Hello,
Small question about this PR - what is the reason behind commit [b21abd5](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4735/commits/b21abd51a77e4fb091bd788a786fd0e2c0031af4) - disable the feature on mobile? It might make a difference for an app I'm working on, and I disabled it on a local branch and it seems to be working as expected.

Thanks,
Avi
author: yeger00

body:
> Hello, Small question about this PR - what is the reason behind commit [b21abd5](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4735/commits/b21abd51a77e4fb091bd788a786fd0e2c0031af4) - disable the feature on mobile? It might make a difference for an app I'm working on, and I disabled it on a local branch and it seems to be working as expected.
> 
> Thanks, Avi

@yeger00 the only reason for not making the element clickable in mobile was since there isn't any tooltip when you hover over the item so it might not be very intutive that it would open a link as soon as you click on it.
Would like to know your reasoning behind enabling it on mobile and based on that we can surely revisit this :)
author: ad1992

body:
@ad1992  Thank you for your comment.
This is still a WIP, but I'm using Excalidraw as the platform to easily create "roadmap" for training. As an example, I'm working on some general "web development" roadmap [here](https://yeger00.github.io/excalidraw-app/) (and the code is [here](https://github.com/yeger00/excalidraw-app)), but I plan to use it a training platform for newcomers in my work as well.
In this example, I patched both the "click" in mobile, and removed the blue icon for links (as suggested [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6370)).

Let me know what you think.
Thanks,
Avi

@ad1992, I am still not able to navigate to links on mobile, except by clicking on the element to invoke edit mode, and then clicking the link in the popup link editor… however this approach is far less than ideal, if you have an element with a link as part of a group. 

Maybe this is down to precision of my touch and the other elements in the neighborhood. The link icon should have precedence and maybe a slightly larger (transparent) footprint.

I've now played with this in many different settings and devices. I am not sure what I am doing wrong, but I see no effect of this change on my iPad or on my Android. I am trying to tap on the link icon, I also tried with my pen hoping it would be more precise, but nothing. 

The idea is I tap the blue link icon and the page opens? The only thing that works now is double-tapping the element, and the link editor comes up. There I can open the link. But this is not very convenient, especially if the element with the link is part of a group.

body:
wait with the merge actually — I forgot when we were discussing this with @ad1992, we might not want to allow the threshold when clicking on _elements_ in view mode as it could have too much false positives when moving around canvas.

IOW we should apply the threshold condition only when clicking on the link icons.
author: dwelle

body:
> wait with the merge actually — I forgot when we were discussing this with @ad1992, we might not want to allow the threshold when clicking on _elements_ in view mode as it could have too much false positives when moving around canvas.
> 
> IOW we should apply the threshold condition only when clicking on the link icons.

Yep I would suggest lets have the threshold only for mobile instead as this issue is in mobile
author: ad1992

body:
> Yep I would suggest lets have the threshold only for mobile instead as this issue is in mobile

Does `isMobile` return true for an iPad? Maybe it should be `!isDesktop`, if we have such a thing.
author: zsviczian

body:
> > Yep I would suggest lets have the threshold only for mobile instead as this issue is in mobile
> 
> Does `isMobile` return true for an iPad? Maybe it should be `!isDesktop`, if we have such a thing.

No actually just checked, so in that case lets have the threshold for "non view mode" and "mobile"
author: ad1992

body:
@dwelle, @ad1992: I think this is now ready. 

I simply removed the strict pointerDown/Up check because 
1. I see no negative effects of checking only for DRAGGING_THRESHOLD, and 
2. to avoid the issue with a precise check for all touchscreens, not just iPads. I tested the solution with my Lenovo ThinkPad Windows laptop and my Chromebook Due tablet as well. Both have touch screens. The links did not work with the strict check on either of them, and they work great with this PR.

I removed the check for isMobile from `handleCanvasPointerUp` for the same reason.
author: zsviczian

body:
> @dwelle, @ad1992: I think this is now ready.
> 
> I simply removed the strict pointerDown/Up check because
> 
> 1. I see no negative effects of checking only for DRAGGING_THRESHOLD, and

 We want to avoid using `DRAGGING_THRESHOLD` unless its view mode since it becomes annoying during view mode in desktop when link opens but the intention is to move the element hence in this case strict check is preferred
 
![Untitled_ Feb 9, 2022 11_48 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11256141/153133634-58903528-ea9f-4a61-bc63-288dd6ecee74.gif)

> 2. to avoid the issue with a precise check for all touchscreens, not just iPads. I tested the solution with my Lenovo ThinkPad Windows laptop and my Chromebook Due tablet as well. Both have touch screens. The links did not work with the strict check on either of them, and they work great with this PR.
> 
> I removed the check for isMobile from `handleCanvasPointerUp` for the same reason.

It would be better to skip the computing if `hitlinkElement` already exists which would be present in case of desktop

The link icon not taking account zoom was intentional, because it's more of a UI element rather than part of the canvas.

This change also doesn't really address https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/418
author: dwelle

body:
> entional, because it's more of a UI element rather than part of the canvas.
> 
> This change also doesn't really

Treating the link icon as a UI element is questionable. While I do not disagree that it has a UI function, its appearance will impact the image on screen.
 
I think this does solve the issue, at least partly, since the issue there is with the relative size of the link icons due to the zoom level. Look at the two images attached to the issue.

![Screenshot 2022-02-09 152357](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/95326157/153141871-8d1d2efc-7614-4432-aa7b-15c1e1f9535a.png)

body:
Hi @ad1992, @dwelle,
I wanted to bring this PR to your attention once more. This is an easy fix for tablets, where, if you start to create a multielement with two fingers, there is no way to stop editing because the finalize button is not displayed. The finalize button is only displayed when isMobile is true.
If you'd want to hide the finalize button for non-touch screens, we could implement the isTouchScreen approach here as well. However, I don't see the negative effect of displaying the finalize check button on non-touchscreens.
author: zsviczian

body: Sure lets show this for `isTouchScreen`, for desktop devices its not needed so lets not add it and keep the UI minimal
author: ad1992

body:
@zsviczian sorry for missing this. I somewhat agree with @ad1992 — while I think we could leave it for desktop just fine (it's contextual), the UX isn't the best when using a mouse or any pointing device which actually moves the cursor across canvas, because it will preview the last point all the way until you click the button.

That said, maybe the bad UX is worth the added clarity for those who would otherwise be stuck in the creation mode (i.e. those who don't read tooltips or cannot use keyboard for some reason. Also those whom we incorrectly detect as `!isTouchScreen`)
author: dwelle

body:
> @zsviczian sorry for missing this. I somewhat agree with @ad1992 — while I think we could leave it for desktop just fine (it's contextual), the UX isn't the best when using a mouse or any pointing device which actually moves the cursor across canvas, because it will preview the last point all the way until you click the button.
> 
> That said, maybe the bad UX is worth the added clarity for those who would otherwise be stuck in the creation mode (i.e. those who don't read tooltips or cannot use keyboard for some reason. Also those whom we incorrectly detect as `!isTouchScreen`)

As a user I just click on the same point to stop editing. As per the tooltips, if people are using multi point editing, there is a high chance they would have read the tooltip to get started otherwise its a tendency to draw a line when not reading tooltip. Also we shouldn't add another action coz we are assuming that user might miss tooltips and instead make the tooltip better, and the space could be utilised for hidden actions which are available only via keyboard eg "zoom to fit" so for desktop users I still think we should avoid showing it unless there is an explicit need
author: ad1992

body:
> As a user I just click on the same point to stop editing. As per the tooltips, if people are using multi point editing, there is a high chance they would have read the tooltip to get started otherwise its a tendency to draw a line when not reading tooltip. Also we shouldn't add another action coz we are assuming that user might miss tooltips and instead make the tooltip better, and the space could be utilised for hidden actions which are available only via keyboard eg "zoom to fit" so for desktop users I still think we should avoid showing it unless there is an explicit need

So I am one of those users who missed reading the tooltip 😳 and struggled to finalize the line I started with two fingers on my iPad... Why do we have the finalize button on the mobile toolbar? BTW I missed that as well, until @dwelle brought it to my attention. In short the learning might be that I should pay more attention to tooltips, but also that this function is somehow not intuitive (at least not for me)... I will make the change to only show the finalize button if the user initiated the action with a touch.
author: zsviczian

body:
> > As a user I just click on the same point to stop editing. As per the tooltips, if people are using multi point editing, there is a high chance they would have read the tooltip to get started otherwise its a tendency to draw a line when not reading tooltip. Also we shouldn't add another action coz we are assuming that user might miss tooltips and instead make the tooltip better, and the space could be utilised for hidden actions which are available only via keyboard eg "zoom to fit" so for desktop users I still think we should avoid showing it unless there is an explicit need
> 
> So I am one of those users who missed reading the tooltip 😳 and struggled to finalize the line I started with two fingers on my iPad... Why do we have the finalize button on the mobile toolbar? BTW I missed that as well, until @dwelle brought it to my attention. In short the learning might be that I should pay more attention to tooltips, but also that this function is somehow not intuitive (at least not for me)... I will make the change to only show the finalize button if the user initiated the action with a touch.

Yes definitely the tooltips are not great right now and could be easily missed thats why we should make the tooltips better for sure and we will soon as well :). What I meant is on Desktop there is a very less chance that user would start multi point without looking at tooltip as the general tendency is to draw a line otherwise I feel. And I do agree the finalise button is not a great UX as well so once we redesign the editor this will be taken care as well.
author: ad1992

body:
The double-tap to finalize isn't a good UX on mobile/touch, so we'll want the finalize button no matter if people read tooltips or not.

Though I must say the current positioning of the finalize button next to the undo buttons will most likely be missed by users too.
author: dwelle

body:
> The double-tap to finalize isn't a good UX on mobile/touch, so we'll want the finalize button no matter if people read tooltips or not.
> 
> Though I must say the current positioning of the finalize button next to the undo buttons will most likely be missed by users too.

Yes thats why I said lets have the finalise button (current one) for mobile/touch devices only as I don't see the need for desktop devices, later on when we redesign we can have it for all devices if needed

author: zsviczian

body:
So this is my proposed solution.

The finalize button is only displayed if the user is working on a touchscreen device (and only after the first touch event)...

I replaced IsMobileContext with DeviceInfoContext. DeviceInfo has currently two values: isMobile and isTouchScreen. In a next PR I could also add penDetected to DeviceInfo, thus moving penDetected out of AppState.
author: zsviczian

body: @ad1992 not sure if you spotted my update. Please, when you have few minutes, take a look and let me know if you agree with the change. Thanks!
author: zsviczian

body: @dwelle , seems @ad1992 and I are happy with this change. Do you approve?
author: zsviczian
dwelle

body:
> Mutating the app.deviceType object will make the context not notify observers. It "works" now by virtue of the dependent components being updated by other means, but it makes the API prone to regressions or not working for other components in the future.

@dwelle, Thanks! 

I learned something today.
Please review and let me know if you are now ok to merge this into master. Once this is merged, as a next PR, I will add penMode to device type.

body:
> Is the changelog something that I should be updating? I see that it is referenced in the contributing guide, but seems fairly blank... I can add details there if that is something people would like!

No that's not needed, changelog update is only needed for package related changes under `src/packages/excalidraw`
author: ad1992

body:
Note that the link behavior around non-textual elements isn't ideal as you need to click on the paths themselves, not the area:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/155711936-eb6ab34d-6d66-4a42-964d-2f4ba9663ab3.png)

But we can improve later if needed.
author: dwelle

body:
Yeah, it's not ideal, I think we could get away with making a rectangle around the element and adding a link to that (that seems to be the pervasive wisdom on stackoverflow on how to handle this). 

I think at that point, people should just make those elements themselves (for your example, add a rectangle behind the one you made) - we could do it for them, but this could end up being frustrating or inconsistent (especially with hand-drawn shapes or circles).
author: JRJurman

body: Thinking about it a little more, we might be able to copy the shape make it transparent with a fill, and wrap that in a link, that should work for most objects, but definitely complicates the logic here.
author: JRJurman

body:
> Thinking about it a little more, we might be able to copy the shape make it transparent with a fill, and wrap that in a link, that should work for most objects, but definitely complicates the logic here.

Yeah. Let's merge what we got and if we get feedback we can improve.

Hmph. It seems we haven't release utils in quite a while. I'll take a look over the weekend.

body:
There's a feedback where if you press/release the `shift` it can keep resizing the text every time. I think it should revert the fontSize upon shift release — if that's doable. I haven't thought about it deep.

![excal-shift-resize-scale-sticky-text](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/154975232-cb7ca2fd-50d3-40aa-abf6-b3e2d737c767.gif)

author: ad1992

body:
I'd rename `verticalAlign` to `textVerticalAlign`, since the former is too generic.

A few bugs:

1. the text align icons don't have active state when wysiwyg active
2. the text align is reset to `top` after you edit
3. bottom vertical align acts incorrectly in specific cases, such as this:
	  1. create a sticky note of single line of text, and exit wysiwyg
	  2. set vertical align to bottom, and horizontal align to left or right
	  3. edit again, and add multiple new lines
	  
	  ![vertical_align_bottom_bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/156341568-509c5cd6-fb3a-4527-890a-c252d697b10a.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
> I'd rename `verticalAlign` to `textVerticalAlign`, since the former is too generic.
> 
> A few bugs:
> 
> 1. the text align icons don't have active state when wysiwyg active
> 2. the text align is reset to `top` after you edit
> 3. bottom vertical align acts incorrectly in specific cases, such as this:
>    
>    1. create a sticky note of single line of text, and exit wysiwyg
>    2. set vertical align to bottom, and horizontal align to left or right
>    3. edit again, and add multiple new lines
>    
>    ![vertical_align_bottom_bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/156341568-509c5cd6-fb3a-4527-890a-c252d697b10a.gif)

I have pushed fix for the above issues, can you try now ?
author: ad1992

body: > > > Updated the icons to ☝🏻 one weird thing though, the vertical-align icons for right and center look different in your video @dwelle, any idea why ?

author: dwelle

body:
Cool!

One issue though: if you're moving your pointer moderately fast, it tends to miss a lot of elements you cross over.

![excal-eraser-speed-bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/157274780-a1864ccf-bca0-49f1-83f3-e4faebd7ccf4.gif)

I haven't checked the code, but I assume it's because you're sampling just the positions you get from the actual `pointermove` events.

One thing we can do is to interpolate more positions on the line between actual pointer events, to get more position samples:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/157274673-0fdfb11a-213d-45c0-a1ad-3c4d7591611b.png)
author: dwelle

body:
A few notes on the implem:

- the `lastScenePointer` should be in `pointerDownState` as it should only relate to a state while pointerdown is active. This will also solve a bug where the `lastScenePointer` is incorrectly leaking into next pointerdown events (e.g. you click somewhere, then click on the other side of the canvas and it thinks you've moved your move from A to B while in `pointerdown` which isn't true)
- the `this.handleEraser` logic should be handled inside `onPointerMoveFromPointerDownHandler` instead of `handleCanvasPointerMove` since we generally reserve the latter for hover states and other non-destructive actions (though it's a bit of a mess right now). The `pointerDownState` is accessible from `onPointerMoveFromPointerDownHandler` anyway.
- likewise, the removal should be moved to `onPointerUpFromPointerDownHandler`.
- having done that, I'd move the `elementIdsToErase` to `pointerDownState` as well since it should be an ephemeral state
author: dwelle

body:
There's a bug where if you select the eraser tool _while_ you have elements selected, then erase an element and undo the change, you end up with the original selection while the eraser tool is still active:

![eraser_selection_undo_bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/157747176-23674e56-fae3-4d92-9075-dcd075af3fc0.gif)

---

Ad cursor image: I think we'll need to either 1) use a circle that has the radius of the hitbox threshold 2) reduce the hitbox threshold to something tiny. Right now the cursor is a pointer which suggests large precision, but the threshold makes it low-precision:

![eraser_radius_precision_issue](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/157747603-845dda2f-ba97-43a3-acc6-48d2eeff6780.gif)

---

Shouldn't we show the eraser tool in zen mode?

author: vercel

body: Hi @ad1992, when you have few minutes... I would really appreciate your thoughts on this. This PR solves a very practical issue for me: I typically use sticky notes with different border and font colors. Currently it is largely impossible to set different colors on a mobile, and it is also not very intuitive on a desktop. I think this is a straight forward / simple solution. I also don't think this is adding much complexity to the UI - though sticky notes are already the most complex.  Let me know what you think.
author: zsviczian

body:
Hi @zsviczian I do agree the mobile bug is somewhat time-consuming and not sure if there is a proper fix for it. 
But more than that it is also not intuitive to set different text colors for bound text. I like the solution of having a different tool for setting text color and maybe we should rename it to "Text color" instead of "Font color"?
What I am wondering is If we later support multiple bound texts then how will we support different text colors for the bound text?
author: ad1992

body:
@ad1992, I opted for "Font color" because the other controls on the panel are "Font size" and "Font family", but I also recognize, that it is "Text align" and not "Font align"... so I am also good with "Text color" instead.

I am not sure how the multiple text elements bound to a container scenario would work, and/or what would be the use case. Instead of multiple text elements bound to the same container I would rather consider supporting formatted text (markdown?) in text elements. That would be a game changer!
author: zsviczian

body:
I'm not sure how to handle this, but I see several problems with the proposed solution:

1. It goes against the current behavior where we set all "identical" properties together, making it inconsistent.
2. I would argue that it's more common to align the outline and the text color of a sticky note, so splitting it in two will add friction from the other side.
3. It adds one more option to the sidebar, increasing UI complexity.
author: dwelle

body:
@dwelle

> It goes against the current behavior where we set all "identical" properties together, making it inconsistent.

I can see the inconsistency, but I also see the current usability challenge.

Because sticky notes handle text wrapping, it is a frequent choice for multi-line text, in which case the stroke color of the container element is transparent, while the stroke color of the text is not.

What would be your alternative proposal? On a mobile device currently it is impossible to set a different color for the text compared to the sticky note's border. I had another PR to solve this issue (#4897), but in the end it only worked on a desktop... unfortunately the mobile keyboard popup triggered editable.onblur (an assumption I wasn't able to fully confirm) and I couldn't find a way around it.

> I would argue that it's more common to align the outline and the text color of a sticky note, so splitting it in two will add friction from the other side.

This we could solve by updating the text stroke color with the border stroke color as long as their colors match.

> It adds one more option to the sidebar, increasing UI complexity.

This I also agree with, however, my view is different. It simplifies a currently very un-intuitive and on mobiles not even working UI, where you need to start editing the text and then select the color property to change the text color... In that sense the more complex UI is still a simplification.
author: zsviczian

body:
> Because sticky notes handle text wrapping, it is a frequent choice for multi-line text, in which case the stroke color of the container element is transparent, while the stroke color of the text is not.

Yes, in my experience this is the main reason for this, but we'll be adding a separate support for wrapping text outside of sticky notes: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4267

> This I also agree with, however, my view is different. It simplifies a currently very un-intuitive and on mobiles not even working UI, where you need to start editing the text and then select the color property to change the text color... In that sense the more complex UI is still a simplification.

I wouldn't say our behavior is unintuitive, it's just opinionated. If a user does need the extra customization, they'll need to do it from the wysiwyg editor.

As for how to make it work on mobile, that's a separate question.
author: dwelle

body:
This would be an alternative solution… choosing which element type (or all) you want to edit. This would have benefits elsewhere as well. e.g. you could select all and change the color of only the arrows…

What do you think?

![B9F93191-B027-4A86-B547-9899623F406C](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/158623782-148fe8ab-cc0e-411e-b368-50a90e0585ad.jpeg)
author: zsviczian

body:
@dwelle What do you think of this setup?
Comes with the same benefit as the dropdown list... i.e. I can edit just the colors of the arrows, or just the text, etc. in my scene.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/158754236-1397f674-6741-4ff4-8b3b-d301116c9dab.png)

or maybe the tabs fit better vertically:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/158759578-58f802cb-6ad2-4b0c-89ef-98a904ac4d98.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/158759723-e60b9a63-2e13-402b-8e9f-50286f6742d7.png)

And we would only display the tabs relevant for the selection:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/158760094-8871bdfd-0fcb-46ca-bc54-e9636576d863.png)
author: zsviczian

body:
Thinking again updating properties of bound text is indeed a valid and good usecase, I think the best solution will be to add the text area tool (which we will be adding very soon) and that would help in solving this problem in the right and efficient way without complicating the UI so we might want to avoid adding the text color as that would complicate the UI and not add much value once text area tool is live. As per the suggestion for showing the tabs for drawings that need to be updated, this would complicate the UI lot more imo and not really a genuine use case as such.
To temporarily solve the issue in mobile specifically do you think if we allow to `bind` via context menu that could help @zsviczian? Though it would add friction coz of the flow unbind -> update props -> bind but still will work which isn't possible at all right now.
author: ad1992

body: I agree that all these options add complexity to the UI. I'll drop this for now and wait for the text area tool.
author: zsviczian

author: 9kartik

body:
Filling a larger area seems to be a serious resource drag. What about filling it less dense?
The fill does not work on iOS and Android if the box is a bit larger. 
I notice the demo is with 590% zoom. I recommend testing and optimizing for 100% zoom.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/159656665-12de0a15-2662-4218-bd4c-fdcf6062b4b7.mp4

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/159656764-9041fb0b-1da9-41ea-9765-bd14da73578b.MOV
author: zsviczian

body:
Yes, I noticed the performance issues too.

But, the more important factor here is the feature itself — right now we want to be very careful what new features we introduce, and each must be clearly justified and weighed against other features we might wanna add in the future.

Introducing a new fill pattern is a big deal, and I'm not sure if we want to add a new one right now, especially without previous consideration.
author: dwelle

body:
(Hi @dwelle @zsviczian @ad1992 , I believe I should have started this as a feature request first before raising this PR 😅)

I felt this pattern was provided out of the box from roughjs and seemed like a good feature to have.
I was thinking as a user that  when we represent sets - having dots as a fill pattern would appear more impactful while we intersect and also would show density.

Thinking out loud here : Maybe after when I fix this performance issue, we can roll it out and check from analytics how many people actually use it and whether it is a hit or not. If not, we can roll it back. What do you think?

PS: I'm totally okay with parking this if such features are not in your roadmap.
author: 9kartik

body:
> Thinking out loud here : Maybe after when I fix this performance issue, we can roll it out and check from analytics how many people actually use it and whether it is a hit or not. If not, we can roll it back. What do you think?

This must be a design decision = what effect it will have on the product and does it fit into the product strategy?

Moreover, we don't want to be shipping functional features only to unship them in a month's time if they don't get enough usage. More still, even if the feature garners reasonable popularity, it would still (potentially) prevent a different feature being shipped in its place (= you'd likely be stuck in a local optimum).
author: dwelle

body: @dwelle cool, I understand. Will close this. 🙂

zsviczian

body:
> attempting to detect if the user is using a pen in ShapesSwitcher. My solution seems to work on my desktop on my Android phone, but not on my iPad. What am I doing wrong?

Haven't checked yet, but before we start investigating, what's the reasoning behind the change? I often pick tools with my fingers before starting to draw with a pen. (EDIT: I noticed the underlying issue https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4936, so let's continue discussing there)
author: dwelle

body:
@dwelle,
#4936 is the underlying issue + moving penDetected into DeviceType context because it makes more sense next to isMobile and isTouchscreen.

BTW I think I found the issue on iOS. Based on my testing it would appear that on Safari the label's onPointerUp fires before the input's onChange. 

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/3b37ffbf6ecff8e2cce85ebe53c366795d0fe00a/src/components/ToolButton.tsx#L151-L159

On Safari lastPointerTypeRef.current is null by the time onChange is called... 

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/3b37ffbf6ecff8e2cce85ebe53c366795d0fe00a/src/components/ToolButton.tsx#L169-L171

but if I call the ToolButton onPointerDown from label onPointerDown, then PointerType is properly passed. 

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/3b37ffbf6ecff8e2cce85ebe53c366795d0fe00a/src/components/Actions.tsx#L220-L225

I wonder if this is an issue for onImageAction in ToolButton on iOS?

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/3b37ffbf6ecff8e2cce85ebe53c366795d0fe00a/src/components/Actions.tsx#L232-L235

body:
I merged the `onChange` and `onPointerDown` handlers as we should run the same code on either event, if the other doesn't fire for some reason.

Otherwise, works well. Thanks!
author: dwelle

body:
> I merged the onChange and onPointerDown handlers as we should run the same code on either event, if the other doesn't fire for some reason.

@dwelle,

Read my comments here: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4943#issuecomment-1074228948

It is not by mistake that I had them separate. the pointerType is null in the onChange on iOS. I read some related articles from 2019 online, that this is an iOS Safari "speciality".
author: zsviczian

body:
ah... I should have looked at the code first... I now understand.
Tested the updated version on iOS, Android and Windows. Works great.

body: How does this PR relate to the custom text-elements feature in #3915?  There appears to be some duplication with #3915.
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
> #3915

@DanielJGeiger the goal of this feature host will be able to render any custom element (with SVG renderer), your one is specific to `Text` custom elements right? However, we would want to support any type of custom elements with the help of this prop in the future so it's generic for all.
author: ad1992

body:
> > #3915
> 
> @DanielJGeiger the goal of this feature host will be able to render any custom element (with SVG renderer), your one is specific to `Text` custom elements right? However, we would want to support any type of custom elements with the help of this prop in the future so it's generic for all.

@ad1992 #3915 is specific to `Text` custom elements, yes.  Are you saying that using #4963 for all custom elements, including `Text`, is better than having a specific system for `Text`?

I have been using #2993 in the classes I teach.  This has given me an appreciation for rendering aspects which are unique to `Text` elements.  For example, I believe `Text` elements should not have to use SVG rendering.  Also, editing `Text` elements, such as `Math`, can lack the WYSIWYG nature of modifying more general elements.  (See also #4906).

Perhaps we could align #4963 and #3915 to share a common API?
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
I am thinking of using this feature for integrating fontAwesome into Excalidraw. I have the following flow in mind:
- When the user clicks and holds the custom element tool then a callback is triggered. In the callback I open a dialog for the user to select the fontAwesome character and then return the SVG and the a text describing the selected element (e.g. "fas fa-birthday-cake") to the custom element. We could call this text `elementSpecification`.
- When the user draws the element using the tool the next time, it will draw the selected fontAwesome SVG. 
- When the user clicks the element in the scene, I would use the onElementClick callback to offer the user to change the fontAwesome font. For this I would need the `elementSpecification`.

The same flow could work for LaTeX equations, or even for Mermaid process charts and a lot more use cases.

I already have a similar flow in Obsidian for embedding LaTeX and Markdown pages. Today I simply use imageElement for this purpose and store specification outside the Excalidraw JSON in the encapsulating Excalidarw markdown file. See a quick demonstration here for the LaTeX usecase: https://youtu.be/r08wk-58DPk?t=15
author: zsviczian

body: @ad1992 @dwelle In your view, what is blocking #2993 from being merged?  #2993 has been open for over a year, and is mature enough that I have used it in my teaching for several semesters.  Requests for you to merge it include [@zsviczian](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/131#issuecomment-906601461), [@baptiste](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/2993#issuecomment-1004713045), [@mcaruel](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/2993#issuecomment-1003520177), [@pschyrem](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/2993#issuecomment-794425207), [@hengsok](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/2993#issuecomment-1046758058).  See also a [review](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/1176#issuecomment-1025213626) by @baptiste.  Perhaps #2993 could be rebased upon #4963.
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
Hi @ad1992, I'd be interested on your thoughts regarding this: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4963#issuecomment-1081479135

I would very much support adding `elementSpecification` to custom elements.
author: zsviczian

body:
@ad1992 @dwelle
The following illustrates a problem I have found when custom `Text` elements use SVG rendering.  In Firefox, paste
```
{"type":"excalidraw/clipboard","elements":[{"type":"text","version":1,"versionNonce":1,"isDeleted":false,"id":"subtype-text","fillStyle":"hachure","strokeWidth":1,"strokeStyle":"solid","roughness":1,"opacity":100,"angle":0,"x":300,"y":300,"strokeColor":"#000000","backgroundColor":"transparent","width":381,"height":24,"seed":0,"groupIds":[],"strokeSharpness":"sharp","boundElements":[],"updated":0,"link":null,"fontSize":20,"fontFamily":2,"text":"A quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.","baseline":17,"textAlign":"left","verticalAlign":"top","containerId":null,"originalText":"A quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.","subtype":"text"},{"type":"text","version":1,"versionNonce":1,"isDeleted":false,"id":"subtype-math","fillStyle":"hachure","strokeWidth":1,"strokeStyle":"solid","roughness":1,"opacity":100,"angle":0,"x":300,"y":330,"strokeColor":"#000000","backgroundColor":"transparent","width":381,"height":24,"seed":0,"groupIds":[],"strokeSharpness":"sharp","boundElements":[],"updated":0,"link":null,"fontSize":20,"fontFamily":2,"text":"A quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.","baseline":17,"textAlign":"left","verticalAlign":"top","containerId":null,"originalText":"A quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.","subtype":"math","useTex":true,"mathOnly":false}],"files":{}}
```
into any preview of #2993 before c3784d0 and into any preview of #2993 from c3784d0 onwards.  In both previews, use the Excalidraw controls to zoom in and out.

The upper element is regular `Text`, and the lower element is `Math`.  Prior to c3784d0, I rendered `Math` elements using the type of SVG renderer used here in #4963.  The rendered text widths differ before c3784d0 but not after.

I believe SVG renderers are appropriate for custom elements unless text rendering is involved.  From my experience teaching with #2993, the renderer in #4963 would need at least two extensions to cover all custom element cases.  First, use `context.fillText()` to render multiple text nodes to the canvas.  Second, permit the custom element to render multiple SVGs to the canvas.  We might also want a post-rendering callback to refresh the scene like #3915 does.

author: DanielJGeiger

body:
When `transformHandles` is `false`, it appears the element can't be selected since we don't render any bounding box, which isn't true. I'd prevent selection, though this can be a problem with how we handle dragging.

One other thing are elements with `stackedOnTop: true`. They can currently be grouped with regular elements and such a group no longer behaves atomically with respect to z-index, which will lead to buggy behavior.

IMO `stackedOnTop` elements should automatically be prevented to be selected (thus grouped with other elements), until we figure out a better solution.

body:
Excited for this to land!

Some notes:

- [ ] `selectAll` action should omit locked elements to align with other apps out there (figma, miro...).
- [ ] you should be able to box-select over a locked element. Meaning, if you start dragging over a locked element, right now it prevents box-selection. Here's Miro:

	![locking-box-selection](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/159912489-f1ff298f-f0a0-44f5-b6c8-6c2ed0350278.gif)

	If this proves too hard, we can leave it for another PR.

	(EDIT: this is actually kinda big now that I think about it. If you lock a background element that all other elements sit on, it would prevent a major part of interaction)

- [ ] cursor shouldn't update for locked elements (right now it shows `move` cursor when hovering over a locked element)

> I've taken the approach right now to only implement locking for single elements, ignoring locking of groups. I think to implement group locking you would need to track the lock state of groups in the app state, unless there is a different way?

I think the way to go is to lock all the elements within a group, and when selecting a group we'd check if all elements within a group are locked → if yes, then prevent group selection.

body:
> > I think the way to go is to lock all the elements within a group, and when selecting a group we'd check if all elements within a group are locked → if yes, then prevent group selection.
> 
> @dwelle Just thinking about this some more, I think this might lead to some confusing behaviour... I can group two elements and then lock one of them.

Not sure at all, just an idea, but maybe this should not be possible...? So, instead it would always be the case that if you lock an element the entire group is locked. I wonder how that would pan out...? 

Put a bit differently, what would be an example situation where you would want to lock one element in a group?
author: chipbite

body:
> Put a bit differently, what would be an example situation where you would want to lock one element in a group?

I think yes. You have a library item or something where it's composed of a background (happens a lot for white-background things) that you don't want moved when editing the group elements.

More generally, I'm not seeing the UX issues. We should follow what Figma is doing:

1. when an element in a group is locked but there are other elements, the group can be selected
2. if such a group is moved, all elements in the group are moved, even the locked ones
3. if all elements in a group are locked, the group can't be selected

body:
> If we're happy then there is only the outstanding item of shortcuts, see the above thread for that 👍

I can take a look at the shortcuts in a bit.

I've noticed a bug around group locks: if you start box-selecting over a group element that's locked, from the moment the box selection selects a non-locked element from that group, the group is selected and dragged, jumping around.

I think the issue stems from the group thinking you've started dragging because you initially clicked on a group member even if it was locked. I don't recall our implementation exactly, but it'll likely be related to `pointerDownState` and how we determine if drag has occurred.

Below, the blue rectangle is locked and both are in a group:

![group-lock-drag-bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/160150434-06cf6cba-57ff-4e44-90eb-561bed311155.gif)
